title: PM Lane 321 - Project Miner Temp Power Customer Delivery And Durable Proof Route And Payload Design No-Live Packet
authority_basis: Standing PM blocker authority and repo technical authority, used here to convert Lane 319 and Lane 320 into exact future request contracts without admitting route implementation, persistence, or any execution path.
activation_condition: Lane 320 already defines the future delivery/proof readback contract.
No Temp Power customer delivery and durable proof route-and-payload design packet exists yet.
The next blocker after lane 320 remains design-only and does not yet justify route implementation or runtime admission.
current_truth: Lane 315 remains the hosted-green runtime baseline, and it still blocks actual delivery execution inside the current customer-preview review slice.
Lane 319 defines the future storage plan while Lane 320 defines the paired readback contract; both now support exact future request-contract design.

The next bounded design step is request design only: define the future mutation route, common envelope, required payload fields, success/failure responses, idempotent replay expectations, and mandatory follow-up readback proof.
Route implementation, persistence, and hosted delivery/proof behavior remain separate later packets.
Finance/payroll/billing/invoice/accounting outputs and source writeback remain outside this lane.
allowed_actions: Define the future mutation route placeholder, common envelope requirements, required payload fields, and success/replay/failure expectations for customer delivery and durable proof review.
Preserve visible blocked boundaries for runtime delivery, finance, and source writeback.
not_allowed: No product code, schema, route implementation, or hosted action.
No persistence execution or runtime route admission.
No live or local POST claiming customer delivery or proof recording occurred.
No finance, payroll, billing, invoice, accounting, external finance sync, or source writeback work.
No auth, ingress, DNS, or secret changes.
validation: Decision label, selected outcome, future mutation route, common envelope, required payload fields, follow-up readback proof, and blocked-domain boundaries are present across touched lane 321 files
Canonical PM docs/status route the next follow-on step through a separate execution-gate packet rather than implementation admission
git diff --check passes for touched lane 321 and canonical PM doc surfaces
validation_result: PASS: lane 321 defines the future delivery/proof route and payload contract and keeps implementation, runtime delivery, finance, and source-writeback widening deferred behind a later execution gate.
